pages.raw_text:
(By United Press Leased WIre)


WALLACE, Idaho, Aug.
3. 22.-Burke can not be saved.
The total energies Of every
man who can be summoned
in the district are being di-
rected against the flames
that are wiping out the little
mining town.


Fores Superintendent
Weigle has no hope of saw
ing the town, as practically
all of his fire fighters have
been put out of commission
by the desperate battle of the
last 36 hours.


The negro troops of the
Twenty-fifth regiment are
resting in Wallace, but can
not be pressed into service
until tonight, as they have
been on constant duty since
4 O'Clock Saturday after
noon. Fifty-two Of these
men have rendered some of
the best service in fighting
back the fires from Wallace.
To their efforts it was due
that the onrushing flames on
Placer creek was stemmed
long enough to permit the
residents to make good their
escape.
